pdg_node.informal_desc: Bootstraps the internal state containers for the dynamic stance estimator — allocates covariance matrices (P, Q, R), latent mean/variance buffers, and any persistent bookkeeping needed before the first estimation step.

declaration.name: stance_state_init
declaration.docstring: Bootstraps the internal state containers for the dynamic stance estimator — allocates covariance matrices (P, Q, R), latent mean/variance buffers, and any persistent bookkeeping needed before the first estimation step.
declaration.conceptual_summary: StochasticFilterStateBootstrapper. Allocates and initializes the full set of persistent numeric containers required by a recursive probabilistic estimator — specifically the prior covariance matrix (P), process noise covariance (Q), and observation noise covariance (R) — along with latent mean and variance buffers, seeded from hyperparameter priors supplied at construction time. The operation maps a static configuration descriptor into a typed, immutable state bag that encodes the estimator's initial uncertainty beliefs before any observations are processed. This is a pure setup transform: it performs no inference, applies no data, and produces a deterministic zero-step posterior ready to receive the first update tick.. Applications: Robotics / SLAM: Initializing the covariance and noise matrices of an Extended Kalman Filter before the first LiDAR or IMU observation is fused into a robot's pose estimate — identical P/Q/R allocation pattern with a 6-DOF latent state vector, Computational Neuroscience: Bootstrapping a Bayesian brain-state decoder prior to streaming electrophysiological time-series data, where the latent mean buffer represents a neural population vector and R encodes electrode noise characteristics, Quantitative Ecology: Setting up a state-space population dynamics model before ingesting seasonal survey counts, where Q captures environmental stochasticity and P encodes prior uncertainty over hidden carrying-capacity parameters, Structural Health Monitoring: Pre-allocating the filter state for a vibration-based damage detection system on a civil structure, where D encodes the number of tracked modal frequencies and R is calibrated from baseline accelerometer noise floors

pdg_node.informal_desc: Core dynamic-stance estimation pass: consumes the current stance state and a new observation vector, runs the estimation kernel (predict + update), and returns an updated immutable stance state together with the estimated stance output.

declaration.name: stance_estimation
declaration.docstring: Core dynamic-stance estimation pass: consumes the current stance state and a new observation vector, runs the estimation kernel (predict + update), and returns an updated immutable stance state together with the estimated stance output.
declaration.conceptual_summary: ImmutablePosteriorStateUpdater. Consumes an immutable latent-state object and a new observed measurement vector, then executes a two-phase Bayesian estimation kernel — a forward projection of the prior distribution (predict) followed by a likelihood-weighted correction (update) — to produce a new immutable state object and the posterior mean vector. The operation maps the tuple (prior belief, new evidence) → (updated belief, point estimate), implementing a single step of a recursive Bayesian filter without side effects on the input state.. Applications: Robotics & autonomous navigation: sequentially fusing IMU accelerometer/gyroscope readings with wheel-odometry to maintain a posterior estimate of a mobile agent's 6-DOF pose (position + orientation tensor) across discrete control loop ticks, enabling real-time localization without replaying full sensor history., Climate & geophysical modeling: assimilating a sparse vector of satellite radiance observations into a high-dimensional atmospheric state tensor at each forecast cycle to produce an updated posterior estimate of temperature, pressure, and humidity fields — the core step in ensemble Kalman data-assimilation pipelines., Computational neuroscience / brain-computer interfaces: updating a probabilistic model of a subject's intended motor-command vector from a new multi-electrode neural firing-rate snapshot at each millisecond bin, producing a posterior estimate of the latent motor intention for real-time neuroprosthetic decoding., Quantitative signal processing in IoT sensor networks: recursively refining the posterior estimate of a slowly drifting physical process scalar (e.g., structural strain, ambient chemical concentration) from noisy, asynchronous sensor readings, enabling low-latency anomaly detection without batch reprocessing.
